sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.1 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: An element$g$of a group$G$is a non-near generator of$G$if for every subset$S G$such that$|G: g,S |<$it follows that$|G: S |<$. The set of all non-near generators of$G$forms a characteristic subgroup of$G$called the lower near Frattini subgroup of$G$, denoted by$(G)$. A~subgroup$M G$is nearly maximal in$G$if it is maximal with respect to being of infinite index in$G$. The intersection of all nearly...
